name: Para (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the equatorial heat and the blood of those who fought for the state

description: A white field crossed by a red diagonal band running from the upper hoist to the lower fly, charged with a single white five-pointed star at its centre.
history: Adopted in 1889, just after the Proclamation of the Republic; the single star represents Spica, the same star that stands for Para on the celestial sphere of the Brazilian national flag because Para lies north of the Equator.

features.symbolism: A red diagonal band across a white field carrying a single white star — representing Para north of the Equator
Spica (Alpha Virginis), the star representing Para in the celestial sphere of the Brazilian national flag, as it lies above the Equator
